title: senecio melastomifolius subsp. longibracteatus
intro: Senecio melastomifolius subsp. longibracteatus, commonly known as Longbracted Jacob's Coat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine for its properties in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Longbracted Jacob's Coat is traditionally prepared as a tea or tincture for medicinal purposes, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
